title: Get a T1 income tax package - Personal income tax - Canada.ca
content:
## Get a T1 income tax package
You may be looking for:
- Filing a paper tax return
- Filing a return with tax software
If you are filing a return with tax software, you do not have to get the income tax package. Use the income tax package only if you are filing a paper return or need tax forms or schedules.
You can get the tax package in one of the following ways:
Use the income tax package for the province or territory where you lived on December 31, 2025 .
- Newfoundland and Labrador
- Northwest Territories
- Prince Edward Island
- Outside Canada (non-residents and deemed residents of Canada)
If you are not sure which tax package may apply to you, you can find out which package to use for special situations .
Depending on your tax situation, you may need additional forms, schedules, or publications to help complete your tax return .

## Previous tax packages
For older tax packages, refer to All personal income tax packages .

## Request a tax package by mail
Starting with the 2025 tax year, the Canada Revenue Agency (CRA) will no longer automatically mail the income tax package to certain individuals. Some federal and provincial or territorial schedules have also been removed from the mailed tax package.
- Schedule 2, Federal Amounts Transferred from your Spouse or Common-Law Partner Schedule (S2), Provincial or Territorial Amounts Transferred from your Spouse or Common-Law Partner (except QC)
- Schedule (S2), Provincial or Territorial Amounts Transferred from your Spouse or Common-Law Partner (except QC)
- Schedule 3, Capital Gains or Losses
- Schedule 5, Amounts for Spouse or Common-Law Partner and Dependants
- Schedule 6, Canada Workers Benefit
- Schedule 7, RRSP, PRPP, and SPP Contributions and Transfers, and HBP and LLP Activities
- Schedule 9, Donations and Gifts
- Schedule 11, Federal Tuition Amounts and Canada Training Credit Schedule (S11), Provincial or Territorial Tuition, Education, and Textbook Amounts (except QC)
- Schedule (S11), Provincial or Territorial Tuition, Education, and Textbook Amounts (except QC)
- Schedule 12, Multigenerational Home Renovation Tax Credit Schedule (S12), Provincial Home Renovation Tax Credit (for BC, NB, and SK)
- Schedule (S12), Provincial Home Renovation Tax Credit (for BC, NB, and SK)
- Schedule 13, Employment Insurance Premiums on Self-Employment and Other Eligible Earnings
- Schedule 15, FHSA Contributions, Transfers, and Activities
If you need a tax package or any of these schedules, additional forms, or publications not included in the package, you can request them using one of the following methods:
- Online form Use the appropriate online form to make your request. Refer to: Order forms and publications
Use the appropriate online form to make your request.
Refer to: Order forms and publications
- CRA's automated telephone service You will need your Social Insurance Number (SIN). Call 1-855-330-3305 to make your request. From outside Canada, call 1-613-940-8495 (collect calls accepted).

### CRA's automated telephone service
You will need your Social Insurance Number (SIN).
Call 1-855-330-3305 to make your request.

## Tax packages in alternate formats
Tax packages, forms, and publications are available in:
- Electronic text (e-text)
E-text and large print may be included in the online tax packages. If not, or if you need MP3 or braille, you can order an alternate format .

## Find out which package to use for special situations
- Answer interactive questions
A text version is available if needed.
Choose the situation that applies to you and scroll down for further questions and results.
Based on your selections above:

### Use the T1 income tax package
Use the 2025 income tax package for the province or territory where you resided on December 31, 2025.
For details about benefits and credits available to you, refer to Newcomers to Canada .
Based on your selections above:

### Use the T1 income tax package
Use the 2025 income tax package for the province or territory where you have residential ties.
Based on your selections above:

### Use the T1 income tax package
If you were in Canada for part of the year in 2025 and earned employment income or business income with a permanent establishment in a province or territory of Canada, use the 2025 income tax package for the province or territory where you stayed. You must also complete Form T1248, Schedule D, Information About Your Residency Status .
Based on your selections above:

### Use the income tax package for non-residents and deemed residents of Canada
You may be considered a deemed resident or non-resident of Canada for income tax purposes.
Use the income tax package for non-residents and deemed residents of Canada .
For more information about residency, refer to Determining your residency status .
Based on your selections above:

### Use the T1 income tax package
You may be considered a factual resident of Canada for income tax purposes.
Use the 2025 income tax package for the province or territory where you kept residential ties. You must also complete Form T1248, Schedule D, Information About Your Residency Status .
For details about your tax obligations, refer to Factual residents .
Based on your selections above:

### Use the T1 income tax package
Use the 2025 income tax package for the province or territory where you resided on the date you left Canada.
For details about your tax obligations, refer to Leaving Canada (emigrants) .
Based on your selections above:

### Use the T1 income tax package
Use the 2025 income tax package for the province or territory where the person resided at their time of death.
For instructions on how to file, refer to Doing taxes for someone who died .
Based on your selections above:

### Use the T1 income tax package
They may be considered a factual resident of Canada for income tax purposes.
Use the 2025 income tax package for the province or territory where they had residential ties. You must also complete Form T1248, Schedule D, Information About Your Residency Status .
For details about how to file, refer to Doing taxes for someone who died .
Based on your selections above:

### Use the T1 income tax package
If they earned employment income or business income with a permanent establishment in a province or territory of Canada, complete the 2025 income tax package for the province or territory where they stayed while earning income. You must also complete Form T1248 Schedule D, Information About Your Residency Status .
For details about how to file, refer to Doing taxes for someone who died .
Based on your selections above:

### They may not have tax obligations to Canada
If they had no residential ties with Canada, and did not earn income in Canada, you may not need to file a tax return for them.
For details, refer to Doing taxes for someone who died .
Based on your selections above:

### Use the income tax package for non-residents and deemed residents
You may be considered a deemed resident of Canada for income tax purposes. Use the income tax package for non-residents and deemed residents of Canada .
For more information about residency, refer to Determining your residency status .
Based on your selections above:

### You may not have tax obligations to Canada
If you have no residential ties with Canada, and did not earn income in Canada, you may not have to file a Canadian tax return.
Use this version if you do not want to answer interactive questions .
Some exceptions may apply depending on your residential ties with Canada .
Use the 2025 income tax package for the province or territory where you resided on December 31, 2025.
For more information about benefits and credits that may be available to you, refer to Newcomers to Canada .
Use the 2025 income tax package for the province or territory where you resided on the date you left Canada.
For guidance on your tax obligations as an emigrant, refer to Leaving Canada (emigrants) .
Use the 2025 income tax package for the province or territory where the person resided at their time of death.
For instructions on how to file, refer to Doing taxes for someone who died .
You may be considered a factual resident of Canada for tax purposes.
Use the 2025 income tax package for the province or territory where you kept residential ties. You must also complete Form T1248, Schedule D, Information About Your Residency Status .
For more information, refer to Factual residents temporarily outside Canada .
You may be considered a deemed resident or non-resident of Canada for tax purposes.
Use the income tax package for non-residents and deemed residents of Canada .

### If you earned income in Canada
If you were in Canada for part of 2025 and earned employment income or business income with a permanent establishment in a province or territory of Canada:
- Use the 2025 income tax package for the province or territory where you stayed
- Complete Form T1248, Schedule D, Information About Your Residency Status
Significant residential ties to Canada include:
- A spouse or common-law partner in Canada
- Dependants in Canada
Secondary residential ties that may be relevant include:
- Personal property in Canada, such as a car or furniture
- Social ties in Canada, such as memberships in Canadian recreational or religious organizations
- Economic ties in Canada, such as Canadian bank accounts or credit cards
- A Canadian driver's licence
- Health insurance with a Canadian province or territory
For more information about residency, refer to determining your residency status .

### Give feedback about this page
Thank you for your feedback.
